rag.question: What is the customer refund policy?
rag.ground_truth: Customers can request a refund within 30 days of purchase. Refunds are processed within 5-7 business days.
contexts: Our refund policy allows customers to request refunds within 30 days of purchase. All refunds are processed within 5-7 business days to the original payment method.

rag.question: What plans are available for customers?
rag.ground_truth: We offer three plans: Free, Pro, and Enterprise, each with different feature sets and pricing.
contexts: Available subscription plans include: Free (basic features, limited usage), Pro ($49/month, advanced analytics, priority support), Enterprise (custom pricing, dedicated account manager, SLA guarantee).

rag.question: How does the churn prediction model work?
rag.ground_truth: The churn prediction model uses historical customer behavior, order frequency, and engagement events to compute a risk score between 0 and 1.
contexts: Our churn prediction model analyzes historical order patterns, page visit frequency, session duration, and support ticket volume. Each customer receives a churn risk score (0.0 to 1.0) updated weekly. Scores above 0.7 trigger automated retention campaigns.

rag.question: What events are tracked in the platform?
rag.ground_truth: The platform tracks events including page views, button clicks, form submissions, checkout events, logins, logouts, and search actions.
contexts: The analytics platform captures the following user events: page_view (when a customer views a page), button_click (interaction tracking), form_submit (lead and form completions), checkout (purchase funnel), login/logout (session management), and search (query tracking).

rag.question: What regions does the business operate in?
rag.ground_truth: The business operates in four regions: North, South, East, and West.
contexts: Our customer base is segmented into four geographic regions: North, South, East, and West. Each region has a dedicated sales team and regional performance dashboards.

rag.question: How is customer data protected?
rag.ground_truth: Customer data is protected using AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 in transit. Access is controlled via role-based permissions.
contexts: All customer data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS 1.3. Access to customer data follows the principle of least privilege with role-based access control (RBAC). Annual security audits are conducted by third-party firms.

rag.question: What is the SLA for Enterprise customers?
rag.ground_truth: Enterprise customers receive a 99.9% uptime SLA with 24/7 dedicated support.
contexts: Enterprise plan SLA guarantees 99.9% platform uptime measured monthly. Dedicated 24/7 support with a maximum 2-hour response time for P1 issues. Planned maintenance windows are communicated 72 hours in advance.

rag.question: How are churn risk scores calculated?
rag.ground_truth: Churn risk scores are calculated using ML models that consider order frequency, engagement events, and account age, returning scores between 0 and 1.
contexts: Churn risk scores (0.0 to 1.0) are calculated by a gradient boosting model trained on 18 months of historical data. Features include: days since last order, number of support tickets, average session length, and feature adoption rate. Model is retrained monthly.

rag.question: What payment methods are accepted?
rag.ground_truth: Accepted payment methods include credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), PayPal, and wire transfer for Enterprise accounts.
contexts: We accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and PayPal for Pro plan subscriptions. Enterprise customers may pay via wire transfer or ACH with net-30 terms. All payments are processed through Stripe with PCI-DSS compliance.

rag.question: How can customers upgrade their plan?
rag.ground_truth: Customers can upgrade their plan directly from the billing settings page in their dashboard. Upgrades take effect immediately and are prorated.
contexts: Plan upgrades can be initiated from Settings > Billing > Change Plan. Upgrades are effective immediately with prorated billing for the remainder of the current billing cycle. Downgrades take effect at the next renewal date.

rag.question: What is the data retention policy?
rag.ground_truth: Customer event data is retained for 24 months. Order data is retained for 7 years for compliance purposes.
contexts: Event and behavioral data is retained for 24 months from collection date. Order and financial records are retained for 7 years in compliance with financial regulations. Customers may request data deletion under GDPR/CCPA by contacting support.

rag.question: How do I export my data?
rag.ground_truth: Data can be exported in CSV or JSON format from the dashboard under Settings > Data Export.
contexts: Data export is available under Settings > Data Export. Supported formats: CSV, JSON, and Excel (.xlsx). Export jobs run asynchronously and a download link is emailed upon completion. Large exports may take up to 30 minutes.

rag.question: What is the onboarding process for new customers?
rag.ground_truth: New customers receive a welcome email with setup instructions, followed by a 30-minute onboarding call with a customer success manager.
contexts: Upon signup, new customers receive an automated welcome email with account setup instructions and a link to our Getting Started guide. Pro and Enterprise customers are assigned a Customer Success Manager who schedules a 30-minute onboarding call within 48 hours.

rag.question: Are there API rate limits?
rag.ground_truth: Free plan is limited to 100 API calls/day, Pro to 10,000/day, and Enterprise has no hard limits but is subject to fair use.
contexts: API rate limits by plan: Free = 100 requests/day, Pro = 10,000 requests/day (burst: 100 req/min), Enterprise = unlimited with fair-use policy. Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-*) are returned on all API responses. 429 status is returned when limit is exceeded.

rag.question: What integrations are supported?
rag.ground_truth: The platform integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Zapier, and has a REST API for custom integrations.
contexts: Native integrations include: Salesforce (CRM sync), HubSpot (marketing automation), Slack (alerts and notifications), and Zapier (workflow automation with 5000+ apps). A REST API is available for custom integrations with webhook support for real-time event streaming.

rag.question: What is the cancellation policy?
rag.ground_truth: Customers can cancel anytime. Pro subscriptions continue until end of billing period. No refunds for partial months.
contexts: You may cancel your subscription at any time from Settings > Billing > Cancel Subscription. Pro plan access continues until the end of the current billing period. Monthly subscriptions are non-refundable for the current period. Annual subscriptions are eligible for prorated refunds within 30 days.

rag.question: How does multi-region support work?
rag.ground_truth: Enterprise customers can choose data residency regions. Data is stored in AWS regions with automatic failover.
contexts: Enterprise customers can select their preferred data residency region: US-East, EU-West, or APAC-Southeast. All data, including backups, remains within the selected region. Automatic failover within the same region ensures 99.9% availability. Cross-region replication requires a separate agreement.

rag.question: What support channels are available?
rag.ground_truth: Support is available via email for Free users, live chat and email for Pro, and 24/7 phone plus dedicated Slack channel for Enterprise.
contexts: Support channels by plan: Free = email support (48h response SLA), Pro = live chat + email (4h response SLA, weekdays), Enterprise = 24/7 phone + dedicated Slack channel + email (2h P1 response SLA). A comprehensive knowledge base is available to all users at docs.example.com.

rag.question: How are product updates communicated?
rag.ground_truth: Product updates are communicated via in-app notifications, a changelog page, and monthly email newsletters.
contexts: Product updates are announced through: (1) In-app notification banners at login, (2) Public changelog at changelog.example.com, (3) Monthly 'What's New' newsletter to all active users, (4) Slack #announcements channel for Enterprise customers, (5) Release notes attached to all major version bumps.

rag.question: What analytics dashboards are available?
rag.ground_truth: The platform includes dashboards for customer overview, revenue analytics, churn risk, event funnels, and cohort analysis.
contexts: Built-in dashboards include: Customer Overview (total customers, growth, segmentation), Revenue Analytics (MRR, ARR, average order value), Churn Risk (high-risk customer list, trend charts), Event Funnels (conversion analysis by page flow), and Cohort Analysis (retention curves by signup month). Custom dashboards available for Enterprise.

sql.question: How many customers do we have in total?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_customers FROM customers

sql.question: How many customers are on the Pro plan?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS pro_customers FROM customers WHERE plan = 'Pro'

sql.question: What is the total revenue from completed orders?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS total_revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

sql.question: Which region has the most customers?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT region, COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customers GROUP BY region ORDER BY customer_count DESC LIMIT 1

sql.question: What is the average order amount?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(amount), 2) AS avg_order_amount FROM orders

sql.question: How many customers have a churn risk score above 0.8?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS high_risk_customers FROM customers WHERE churn_risk_score > 0.8

sql.question: List the top 5 customers by total order value
sql.expected_sql: SELECT c.name, ROUND(SUM(o.amount), 2) AS total_spent FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 5

sql.question: How many orders were placed in 2024?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS orders_2024 FROM orders WHERE strftime('%Y', created_at) = '2024'

sql.question: What is the most common churn reason?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT reason, COUNT(*) AS count FROM churn GROUP BY reason ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 1

sql.question: How many events of type 'page_view' occurred?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS page_views FROM events WHERE event_type = 'page_view'

sql.question: What percentage of customers are on the Enterprise plan?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(CASE WHEN plan = 'Enterprise' THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS enterprise_pct FROM customers

sql.question: Which product category generates the most revenue?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT product_category, ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY product_category ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 1

sql.question: How many customers signed up in 2023?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS signups_2023 FROM customers WHERE strftime('%Y', signup_date) = '2023'

sql.question: What is the average churn risk score by region?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT region, ROUND(AVG(churn_risk_score), 4) AS avg_risk FROM customers GROUP BY region ORDER BY avg_risk DESC

sql.question: How many customers have never placed an order?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS no_order_customers FROM customers c WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.customer_id = c.id)

sql.question: Show me the monthly order count for 2024
sql.expected_sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', created_at) AS month, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders WHERE strftime('%Y', created_at) = '2024' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

sql.question: What is the refund rate as a percentage of total orders?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'refunded' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS refund_rate_pct FROM orders

sql.question: Which customers have the highest churn confidence score?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT c.name, ch.confidence_score, ch.reason FROM churn ch JOIN customers c ON ch.customer_id = c.id ORDER BY ch.confidence_score DESC LIMIT 10

sql.question: How many unique sessions are in the events table?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) AS unique_sessions FROM events

sql.question: What is the average number of orders per customer?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers), 2) AS avg_orders_per_customer FROM orders

sql.question: List customers from the North region on the Enterprise plan
sql.expected_sql: SELECT name, email, signup_date, churn_risk_score FROM customers WHERE region = 'North' AND plan = 'Enterprise' ORDER BY churn_risk_score DESC

sql.question: What is the total number of churn predictions?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_churn_predictions FROM churn

sql.question: Which page is visited most often?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT page, COUNT(*) AS visit_count FROM events WHERE event_type = 'page_view' GROUP BY page ORDER BY visit_count DESC LIMIT 1

sql.question: Show revenue by plan type
sql.expected_sql: SELECT c.plan, ROUND(SUM(o.amount), 2) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.plan ORDER BY revenue DESC

sql.question: How many customers are at high churn risk (score > 0.7) per region?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT region, COUNT(*) AS high_risk_count FROM customers WHERE churn_risk_score > 0.7 GROUP BY region ORDER BY high_risk_count DESC

sql.question: What is the total pending order value?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount), 2) AS pending_revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending'

sql.question: How many login events happened in total?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_logins FROM events WHERE event_type = 'login'

sql.question: What is the average confidence score for churn predictions?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(confidence_score), 4) AS avg_confidence FROM churn

sql.question: Which customers signed up most recently?
sql.expected_sql: SELECT name, email, plan, signup_date FROM customers ORDER BY signup_date DESC LIMIT 10

sql.question: Show the distribution of orders by status
sql.expected_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS count, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders), 2) AS pct FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY count DESC
